sources.label: Linear redesign 2024
borrowed: Reduce visual noise while increasing hierarchy density.
Let navigation and chrome recede after orientation is complete.
rejected: Desktop app chrome density that would crowd a transactional patient flow on mobile.

sources.label: Linear refresh 2026
borrowed: Do not compete for attention you have not earned.
Structure should be felt, not seen.
rejected: Extra decorative separators or loud header bars in the booking task area.

sources.label: Next.js preserving UI state
borrowed: Treat nested route state and DOM continuity as first-class responsive behavior.
Preserve local state instead of remounting when layout posture changes.
rejected: Any responsive approach that throws away slot, summary, or drawer state on route navigation.

sources.label: NHS layout guidance
borrowed: Design mobile first.
Use a single-column posture first and work up to wider layouts.
rejected: Desktop-first booking composition that shrinks down without a lawful fold.

sources.label: NHS App web integration
borrowed: An NHS App web integration surfaces a responsive website in the NHS App.
Host-specific changes may include hiding local headers and footers.
rejected: Assuming the embedded host behaves like a full browser tab with all artifact affordances available.

sources.label: NHS App integration standards
borrowed: Embedded services must still evidence WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.
rejected: Treating embedded mode as an accessibility exception.

sources.label: NHS browser differences
borrowed: Browser and app capability differences must be stated truthfully.
rejected: Calm artifact controls that imply calendar or handoff capabilities the host does not provide.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 reflow
borrowed: Non-excepted content should fit a 320 CSS pixel width without two-dimensional scrolling.
rejected: Persistent horizontal overflow caused by rails or sticky trays.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 focus not obscured
borrowed: Author-created sticky content must not hide the focused element.
rejected: Bottom trays that cover focused buttons, fields, or error states.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 focus visible
borrowed: Keep a durable visible focus indicator in keyboard mode.
rejected: Pointer-only affordances for sheet toggles or bottom tray actions.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 target size minimum
borrowed: Touch controls should clear the minimum 24 by 24 CSS pixel target size, with larger transactional targets preferred.
rejected: Compressed chip or tray controls that fall below a touch-safe height.

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 status messages
borrowed: Announce route and state changes without moving focus.
rejected: Responsive changes that only update visible copy without a programmatic status message.

sources.label: WAI ARIA technique ARIA22
borrowed: Use role=status with aria-atomic where route status changes need to be announced.
rejected: Ad hoc aria-live containers with incomplete context.

sources.label: WAI APG modal dialog pattern
borrowed: Move focus to the sheet heading and keep the close action in the tab sequence.
rejected: Non-modal bottom sheets that leave keyboard focus behind the overlay.

sources.label: Playwright emulation
borrowed: Use device presets and viewport overrides to exercise compact, narrow, and wide conditions.
Use touch-enabled device contexts for mobile proof.
rejected: Desktop-only viewport assertions passed off as mobile proof.

sources.label: Playwright page.emulateMedia
borrowed: Use reduced-motion emulation as part of responsive accessibility proof.
rejected: Assuming motion-disabled behavior from static CSS inspection alone.
